epigraph.quote: We walked down to the Island in the Rhine where there was a festival going on. We saw an eel boat which has a beautiful curving shape.

per_stop.quote: It was lovely arriving at Bad Honnef + being welcomed by Annie, Klaus, Patrick + Julia
We had supper - good - at the camp restaurant. Lots of Germans, one Dutch, one Danish. People with dogs.
we saw quaint old buildings, market place, old church with wood & stone carvings & balcony. I sketched this wood carving on the corner wall of a building about 1565.
Charles + Eve swam across the lake. Eve did 2 washes but unfortunately one of the machines was only a drier.
Charles felt a wasp go up his shorts + quickly took them off. The sausage seller was bemused!
The terrace of the house is lovely to sit on, but we have to eat meals indoors because of the wasps.
The week they were liberated, April 11th 1945, the American soldiers made local Weimar people walk through the camp. We saw the ovens.
This is the nicest campsite we've been in. We caught the bus into Bamburg & looked at the cathedral.
It has lovely expensive shops, icecreams + cake shops.
This is the first campsite where Charles has been able to eat with us at the table, instead of hiding in the loo, because there was only one wasp (spheksophobia - fear of wasps) at each meal.
It held 221,725 litres. It's 8.5 m across & 7m high with a dance floor on top.
They've made a little cafe in the Concorde.
Not only no-soup soap, no loo paper, but also no loo seats either, but it clean.
We had supper on the Square + walked down to hear the last post at the Menin Gate.
The campsite had a beautiful view out over the ridge.
